tags_v2.action: weighing soy wax flakes on a scale

seconds_v2.desc: White soy wax flakes are poured from a scoop into a tall aluminum pouring pitcher resting on a white digital kitchen scale reading about 260 g; a large clear bag of wax flakes sits at right, amber fragrance bottles and a black spoon at left on a dark wood table with blue accent light.
More wax flakes tumble into the metal pitcher, the scale climbing toward 270 g, the bulk bag of flakes and dropper bottles framing the workspace.
Flakes continue filling the pitcher, scale now near 280 g; steady measuring of soy wax by weight.
The pour adds more wax, scale reading roughly 296 g, the aluminum jug filling on the scale platform.
Wax flakes keep dropping in, display around 298 g, mounding inside the pitcher.
Pouring continues, the scale passing about 306 g as the pitcher fills with white flakes.
More flakes added, scale near 318 g, the last of this scoop going into the metal jug.
The pour eases off with the scale holding around 318 g, a heap of white wax flakes settled in the pitcher.
Final second: pouring stopped, the aluminum pitcher holds the measured wax flakes on the scale at roughly 318 g, bulk bag and fragrance bottles in view.

seconds_v2.desc: Five empty clear glass mason jars with wicks centered sit in a diagonal row on white tissue paper printed with cursive 'Standley' logo; a stainless steel pouring pitcher tilts in from upper left starting to pour deep red wax into the back jar.
Pour paused; back jar partly filled with translucent red wax, jug held above the row; black plastic spoon and a small clear measuring cup sit on the table to the right.
Pitcher tilts forward, stream of deep red molten wax cascades into the back jar.
Heavier ribbon of red wax pouring; second jar in row also begins receiving wax.
Pour continues, advancing jar-by-jar down the row; first jar full, second jar filling.
Pitcher repositioned slightly; third jar in line beginning to fill with red wax; row of remaining empty jars visible ahead.
Continuous pour; back two jars now full and dark red, third jar half full.
Pitcher moves along the row; third jar nearly full of red wax, fourth jar starting to be filled.
Heavy red wax stream into fourth jar; three filled red jars trail behind, two empty mason jars wait at the front of the row.

seconds_v2.desc: A row of small glass jelly jars lined up diagonally on 'Standley' branded tissue paper; an aluminum pouring pitcher tips deep red molten wax into the jars at the back, wicks held centered by white wick bars, the front jars still empty, blue LED light along the wall.
The pour moves along the row, filling the next jar with red wax up toward the first ring; back jars already filled red, empty clear jars wait at the front, black spoon and a plastic cup at right.
Red wax streams from the metal pitcher into another jelly jar, the line of jars progressively filling, wicks upright on their centering sticks.
Pouring continues into the middle jar, vivid red wax rising inside; the 'Standley' tissue paper beneath catches a few drips.
The pitcher fills the next clear jar with red wax to the ring line, several jars now full at the back of the row.
More red wax is poured down the row, the pitcher angled over an empty jar, wick bars keeping wicks centered.
Pouring progresses toward the front jars, the steady red stream topping each jelly jar to the first ring.
The pitcher continues along, filling another jar with red wax; the row shows a gradient from full red jars to a couple still empty at front.
Final second: red wax poured into the next jar near the front of the row, nearly all the jelly jars now filled to the ring, pitcher still tilted above.

windows.summary_v2: A heat gun is held just above seven freshly poured blush-pink candles in glass jars to smooth and finish their wax tops on branded tissue paper.

tags_v2.action: smoothing candle tops with heat gun
tags_v2.setting: overhead dark wood table with blue LED accent lighting
tags_v2.on_screen_text: Standley (printed on tissue/wrapping paper under the jars)

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead view of seven freshly poured blush-pink candles in straight-sided glass jars arranged on branded 'Standley' tissue paper over a dark wood table; a black heat gun nozzle hovers just above the jars to smooth the wax tops, with bronze lids, black wick-centering bars, white wicks and yellow-handled pliers to the right.
Same arrangement of pink candles; the heat gun is held a few inches above the wax surfaces, gently melting the tops level, blue LED strip glowing along the wall behind the jars.

Heat gun continues hovering over the cluster of pink wax jars; the surfaces of the front candles look glossy and smoothing out, accessories (bronze lids, black centering clips, wicks, pliers) lie on the kraft and tissue paper at right.
The heat gun nozzle shifts slightly over the jars, evenly warming the pink wax tops; seven candles remain set out on the 'Standley' branded paper, finishing the surfaces after pouring.
Continued heat-gun smoothing of the blush-pink candle tops; one front jar's surface shows a small dip near the wick as the warm air levels it, bronze lids and black wick holders staged to the side.
Heat gun held steady above the jar cluster, melting the upper layer of pink wax to remove imperfections; arrangement of jars, lids and tools unchanged on the wood table.
Overhead shot of the pink candles as the heat gun passes over them, the wax tops glistening from the applied heat; blue accent lighting reflects off the glass jars.
Heat gun nozzle remains poised over the front jars, finishing the smooth glossy tops of the pink candles; bronze lids, black centering bars, white wicks and yellow pliers stay grouped on the right.
Final second: the seven blush-pink candles sit with smoothed glossy tops under the hovering heat gun on the 'Standley' tissue paper, completing the heat-gun finishing pass.
